confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Maximum score at Stalinist peak: the Soviet order combined messianic eschatology, revolutionary class identity, and constitutional vanguard monopoly to produce a symbolic order with no legitimate internal challenger. Score applies to peak strength (c. 1936-1953); terminal-phase decay is scored separately in the soviet-union-collapse case. Confidence moderate: requires expansion from founding texts to institutional practices.

reviewNote: Score value 5/5 defensible: collective-immortality-to-sacrifice mechanism (Marxist-Leninist historical materialism, USSR Constitution 1977 collective ownership provisions) supports maximum symbolic-order-strength in the high-period order. Confidence 0.60 reflects three uncertainty factors (chain minimal, founding-text-to-practice gap, wide periodization). Hold at score-review until chain expansion and periodization is bounded to a specific Soviet period.
